anomaly.title: Anomaly Detection Feed
anomaly.subtitle: Real-time network anomalies — Isolation Forest + Statistical models
anomaly.kpiSub: IF = Isolation Forest · Stat = Statistical Z-Score · Consensus = both models agree
anomaly.timelineSub: Purple area = combined anomaly score · Red ● = detected anomaly · Amber = alert threshold (0.70)
anomaly.noTimelineDesc: Select a region with anomaly data
anomaly.driversSub: Top KPI drivers · Severity distribution of detected events
anomaly.topDriversSub: KPIs most frequently flagged as root cause
anomaly.sevSub: High · Medium · Low classification of detected events
anomaly.eventsSub: Sorted by combined score · Only events where both models agree
anomaly.methodSub: How each model works · signal semantics · confidence levels
anomaly.ifDesc: Unsupervised tree-based algorithm. Points requiring fewer random splits to isolate are statistically anomalous — no labeled data required.
anomaly.statDesc: Points beyond 3σ from the rolling mean. Combined with CUSUM for detection of gradual cumulative shifts invisible to standard deviation alone.
anomaly.consensusDesc: Flags only when BOTH models agree — highest confidence, lowest false-positive rate. These are the events NOC engineers should investigate first.

forecast.title: Predictive Forecasting
forecast.subtitle: XGBoost + Prophet 7-day complaint surge forecast
forecast.kpiSub: MAE = Mean Absolute Error (lower = better) · XGBoost wins = best MAE per region
forecast.modelSub: MAE per governorate · Lower bar = more accurate · XGBoost (red) consistently outperforms
forecast.modelChartSub: ARIMA = blue · Prophet = green · XGBoost = red · Lower MAE = better prediction
forecast.winnerSub: Winner per region — model with lowest MAE
forecast.forecastSub: Blue area = actual historical (90 days) · Red dashed = 7-day XGBoost forecast
forecast.noForecastRegion: No forecast data for this region
forecast.scoresSub: Sorted by XGBoost MAE ascending · green = best third · amber = middle · red = worst
forecast.noScores: No score data — re-run Notebook 05 to generate prediction_scores.parquet
forecast.scoresUnavailDesc: Re-run Notebook 05 to generate models/prediction/prediction_scores.parquet — forecast chart and history are still available below.

segments.title: Customer Segmentation
segments.subtitle: K-Means + DBSCAN clustering for targeted QoE interventions
segments.kpiSub: Silhouette ↑ better · Davies-Bouldin ↓ better · PCA variance = 2D representation quality
segments.summarySub: Each cluster represents a distinct customer behavior pattern
segments.vizSub: PCA = 2D dimensionality reduction · Radar = normalised KPI fingerprint per cluster (0–1 scale)
segments.noPCADesc: API offline or kmeans_users.parquet not found
segments.radarSub: Normalised KPI values per cluster (0 = min, 1 = max across all clusters)
segments.noRadar: Not enough KPI columns for radar
segments.kpiProfilesSub: KPI profiles = raw average per cluster · Distribution = % of users per cluster per governorate
segments.kpiProfilesChartSub: Average KPI values — each bar group = one KPI metric
segments.distSub: Stacked % of users per cluster per governorate
segments.tableSub: Full numeric cluster profiles · Columns = top KPI means · sorted by cluster ID

about.heroDesc: An AI-powered network operations intelligence platform developed in collaboration with Huawei Technologies Tunisia. SpiriComp transforms 18 months of Ooredoo Tunisia network data into real-time, actionable NOC intelligence — detecting anomalies, forecasting complaint spikes, classifying root causes, and analyzing customer voice across three languages.
about.pillarsSub: Four core capabilities that shift NOC operations from reactive to predictive.
about.p1Body: Dual-model anomaly detection combines Isolation Forest (unsupervised ML) with Statistical Z-Score + CUSUM. Alerts only fire when both models agree — minimising false positives. Combined anomaly scores, severity classification, and root cause driver identification surface actionable signals for NOC engineers without alert fatigue.
about.p2Body: Three-model ensemble — XGBoost, Prophet, and ARIMA — generates 7-day complaint volume forecasts per governorate. XGBoost consistently achieves the lowest MAE across regions. Forecasts enable proactive resource pre-positioning before complaint surges reach the NOC hotline.
about.p3Title: Network KPIs Mapped to Subscriber Impact
about.p3Body: Every KPI degradation event — RSRP drop, SINR deterioration, throughput collapse — is correlated with real complaint patterns across 24 governorates. SpiriComp surfaces QoE degradation before it generates calls to the NOC hotline, enabling proactive intervention over reactive firefighting.
about.p4Body: The NLP engine classifies customer submissions in Arabic, French, and English simultaneously — no translation pipeline required. Accent-insensitive preprocessing, verb-form lexicons, and a trained sklearn TF-IDF classifier distinguish réclamations from feedback. SQLite stores all submissions with full NLP metadata for NOC dashboard consumption.
about.partnerSub: SpiriComp is the product of a Huawei–operator collaboration framework applied to the Tunisian telecom market.
about.huaweiDetail: Huawei Technologies Tunisia provided enterprise-grade AI/ML methodology, telecom domain expertise, and engineering supervision throughout the SpiriComp development lifecycle. The platform applies Huawei's NOC intelligence best practices — anomaly consensus alerting, QoE-complaint correlation, predictive analytics — to the Tunisian market, bridging global telecom AI standards with local network operations realities.
about.ooredooDetail: Ooredoo Tunisia is Tunisia's leading mobile operator and the source of SpiriComp's production dataset — 50,000+ customer complaints and 552,000 KPI sessions spanning 18 months across 24 governorates and ~4,500 cell sites. All ML models, QoE correlations, and NLP classifiers are trained and validated on real Ooredoo network data.
about.archSub: From raw parquet data to NOC engineer dashboard — five decoupled layers.
about.mlSub: Five production notebooks from raw data ingestion to serialised model artefacts.
about.stackSub: Every component selected for reliability, performance, and telecom-domain fit.
about.docNocDesc: Dashboard features, KPI interpretation, alert workflow for NOC engineers.
about.docKpiDesc: Full KPI and KQI definitions, thresholds, and measurement formulas.
about.docGithubDesc: Full source code — notebooks, FastAPI backend, React dashboard, NLP pipeline.
about.docApiDesc: FastAPI auto-generated docs — all endpoints, schemas, and live examples.
about.docPortalDesc: Multilingual complaint form — Arabic, French, English — for subscribers.
about.docHealthDesc: Live status check — all analytics and NLP endpoints operational status.

landing.heroDesc: SpiriComp ingests and correlates over 50,000 customer complaints with 552,000 network KPI sessions across 4,500–5,000 cell sites and 24 governorates, enabling proactive QoE degradation detection and SLA protection.
landing.infraH2b: hundreds of cell sites across entire regions
landing.infraDesc: SpiriComp delivers one unified view of telecom network health, ingesting real-time KPI data from every cell site across 24 governorates, seamlessly correlated with customer complaints and QoE degradation events.
landing.predictDesc: By forecasting complaint peaks before they materialize, SpiriComp gives NOC engineers the critical lead time needed to act proactively and prevent SLA violations.
landing.bannerDesc: Accelerate issue resolution by correlating network performance data with customer feedback across extensive historical operations.
landing.modGisDesc: Detection of geographic and temporal complaint patterns across the entire network.
landing.modMlDesc: Automated network health monitoring and deviation detection using Isolation Forest.
landing.modAiDesc: Anticipation of customer complaint peaks with actionable operational lead time.
landing.modRcaDesc: Identification of underlying causes linking network performance to customer experience.
landing.modUxDesc: User profiling and K-Means clustering for personalized service improvements.
landing.modNlpDesc: Multilingual text analysis and categorization of customer complaints in 3 languages.
landing.testi1: SpiriComp has transformed our complaint management workflow. Tasks that once required hours of manual correlation are now completed in seconds. The anomaly detection capability has already prevented several critical service incidents.
landing.testi2: The forecasting module is exceptional. We can now anticipate complaint surges days in advance. Our customer satisfaction metrics have shown significant improvement as a direct result of this proactive capability.
landing.testi3: The root cause analysis is remarkably insightful. It not only identifies anomalies — it clearly explains what drives customer complaints. Our engineers value the transparency and actionable insights it provides.
landing.testi4: Before SpiriComp, we operated entirely in reactive mode. Today, we are fully proactive. The automated text analysis instantly categorizes every incoming complaint, giving us real-time visibility into network quality across all regions.
